register.title: Start your free trial
register.subtitle: No credit card required
register.testimonialQuote: Hook0 replaced our homegrown webhook system in a day. Delivery reliability went from 94% to 99.9%.

applications.title: Applications
applications.name: Application name
applications.nameHelpText: Your product or API name. Include the environment, e.g. 'acme-api-production'.
applications.formSubtitle: An application is an isolated environment that contains everything webhook-related.
applications.createTooltip: To continue, you need to add a name for your application.
applications.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete the application {name}? This action cannot be undone.
applications.subtitle: Each application sends events to the Hook0 API. Hook0 dispatches these events to customers through webhooks.

empty.title: No applications
empty.description: Applications group your webhooks and events.

eventTypes.name: Event type name
eventTypes.createSubtitle: Each event sent through a webhook must have an event type.
eventTypes.helpDescription: An event represents something that happened in your application.
eventTypes.helpFormat: Event types should be in the form of:

eventTypes.subtitle: Each event sent through a webhook must have an event type.

empty.description: Define your first event type to start structuring your webhook events.

events.title: Events
events.subtitle: Events received from your application and forwarded to subscriptions as webhooks.
events.sendTestEventSubtitle: To send a test event: first create an event type, then a subscription, then send the event.
events.eventLabelsHelp: Events match subscriptions whose labels are a subset of the event's labels. Events with extra labels still trigger matching subscriptions.
events.invalidEventMessage: The test event form is invalid
events.eventSentMessage: The test event was sent successfully

empty.title: No events received
empty.description: Send your first event using the API.

subscriptions.title: Subscriptions
subscriptions.formSubtitle: A subscription (webhook) receives all events sent to Hook0 that match its filters.
subscriptions.descriptionHelpText: A short name to identify this subscription.
subscriptions.secretDescription: The secret is used to authenticate the webhooks sent by Hook0.
subscriptions.httpEndpointHelp: Hook0 sends webhooks to this URL when events match.
subscriptions.webhookSiteHelp: Need a test URL? Use {link} to get one instantly.
subscriptions.webhookSiteHint: Need a test URL? Use {link} to get one instantly.
subscriptions.headersHint: Custom HTTP headers sent with each webhook request.
subscriptions.metadataHint: Attach custom data to this subscription. Returned when querying the API, useful for tagging or internal tracking.
subscriptions.subscriptionLabelsHelp: Only events with at least these labels will trigger this subscription. Events can have additional labels.
subscriptions.selectEventTypes: Select event types to listen to
subscriptions.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this subscription?
subscriptions.subtitle: Webhook subscriptions that receive events from this application.
subscriptions.disableConfirm: Are you sure you want to disable {name}? All pending and scheduled webhook deliveries will be marked as failed and stay in this state even if you re-enable the subscription later.
subscriptions.label: Label
subscriptions.urlHelp: The URL that will receive webhook payloads
subscriptions.eventTypesFilterHelp: Filter which event types trigger this subscription
subscriptions.headersHelp: Custom headers sent with each webhook request
subscriptions.secretHelp: Used to sign webhook payloads for verification
subscriptions.testCorsWarning: The endpoint may be reachable but blocked by browser CORS policy. Deploy and test via the API for accurate results.
subscriptions.noEventTypes: No event types yet. Create one first.

empty.title: No subscriptions yet
empty.description: Create a webhook subscription to start receiving events.

logs.title: Request Logs
logs.subtitle: Recent webhook delivery logs.
logs.subtitleRetention: Items older than {days} days are not shown.

empty.title: No request attempts yet
empty.description: Request attempts will appear here when events are delivered to subscriptions.

apiKeys.title: API Keys
apiKeys.deleteConfirmNamed: Are you sure you want to delete {name}?
apiKeys.copyWarning: Make sure to copy your API key now. You won't be able to see it again.
apiKeys.subtitle: These keys will allow you to authenticate API requests to Hook0.

empty.title: No API keys
empty.description: Create an API key to authenticate your webhook API requests.

serviceTokens.title: Service Tokens
serviceTokens.simpleDescription: Quickly scope your token to a single application with preset expiry durations.
serviceTokens.advancedDescription: Full control over Biscuit attenuation with custom Datalog claims and detailed token inspection.
serviceTokens.customDatalogClaimsHelp: Add custom Biscuit Datalog checks and rules. Each line should be a valid Datalog statement (e.g., check if time($t), $t < 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z).
serviceTokens.tokenDisplayWarning: This token is shown only once. Copy it now.
serviceTokens.copyWarning: Make sure to copy your token now. You won't be able to see it again.
serviceTokens.subtitle: Service tokens are organization-wide API keys that allow external programs to send API requests to Hook0 without relying on user credentials.
serviceTokens.deleteConfirm:
Are you sure you want to delete the service token {name}?

Every token derived from this token will be revoked as well.
serviceTokens.aiSubtitle: Connect Claude, ChatGPT, or any MCP-compatible AI to manage your webhooks
serviceTokens.aiDescription: With Hook0 MCP Server, you can let AI assistants interact with your webhook infrastructure using natural language:
serviceTokens.aiFeature1: List and inspect events, subscriptions, and delivery history
serviceTokens.aiFeature2: Create and manage webhook subscriptions via conversation
serviceTokens.aiFeature3: Debug failed deliveries and retry them instantly
serviceTokens.aiGetStarted: Create a service token above and use it as your HOOK0_API_TOKEN in your MCP configuration.
serviceTokens.viewDescription: A service token allows you to authenticate any API call related to your organization.
serviceTokens.tokenWarning: This token can do everything in your organization.
serviceTokens.aiConnectDescription: Connect this token to Claude, ChatGPT, or any MCP-compatible AI assistant to manage your webhooks using natural language.
serviceTokens.detailedSetupInstructions: For detailed setup instructions, see the
serviceTokens.attenuateDescription: You can reduce your token's permissions. This will generate and display a new token. Hook0 will only store your root token, not attenuated ones; but if you revoke a root token, every token derived from it will be revoked too.
serviceTokens.reduceScope: Reduce scope to one of your applications
serviceTokens.reduceScopeHelp: By default, tokens can access any of your applications.
serviceTokens.publicKeyError: An error occurred while getting the biscuit public key from Hook0. If the problem persists, please contact support.
serviceTokens.invalidForm: You must select an application or an expiration date
serviceTokens.invalidExpirationDate: The expiration date must be in the future
serviceTokens.tokenGenerated: The service token has been generated
serviceTokens.tokenGenerationError: An error occurred while generating the service token

empty.title: No service tokens
empty.description: Service tokens provide scoped API access for your applications.

widget.applicationDetails: Isolated environment that contains everything webhook-related.

tutorial.title: Get Started with Hook0

welcome.title: Welcome to Hook0!
welcome.description: Let's set up your first webhook pipeline in a few simple steps.

success.title: You're all set!
success.description: Your webhook pipeline is ready. Events will now be delivered to your subscription.

tutorial.step1Description: An organization groups your team and billing plan. Create one to get started.
tutorial.selectExistingOrganization: Select an existing organization already linked to your account
tutorial.continueToApplication: You can now create your first application.
tutorial.step2Description: An application is an isolated environment in Hook0. It has its own event types, events, subscriptions and request attempts. If your plan allows it, you can create multiple applications in order to isolate multiple environments or systems inside the same organization.
tutorial.continueToEventType: You can now create your first event type.
tutorial.selectApplication: You can also select an existing application to continue the tutorial.
tutorial.orgAppIdRequired: Organization ID and Application ID are required
tutorial.somethingWentWrong: Something went wrong. Please try again. If the problem persists, contact support.

intro.title: Welcome to Hook0 - Let's Get Started!
intro.subtitle: This tutorial will help you get started quickly with Hook0.
intro.inThisTutorial: In this tutorial, you will be creating:
intro.skipNote: You can skip this and do it by yourself {link} or using our UI.
intro.splitHeading: Set up your first webhook pipeline
intro.splitDesc: We'll walk you through creating your first organization, application, event type, subscription, and sending a test event. It takes about 3 minutes.

step3.title: Create your first event type
step3.subtitle: Event types categorize your events. Each subscription filters by event type. Hook0 includes the event type as a header in each webhook, so your application knows which payload schema to expect.
step3.canCreateSubscription: You can now create your first subscription.

step4.title: Create a subscription
step4.subtitle: Subscriptions are a way to choose what kind of events you are interested in (depending on their event type and labels) and where to dispatch them as webhooks.
step4.canSendEvent: You can now create your first event.

step5.title: Send an event
step5.subtitle: In this step, you will send a test event. You should make it match your subscription's event type and label so that you receive a webhook!
step5.eventSentMessage: You just sent your first webhook event!

congrats.title: Congratulations on your first steps with Hook0
congrats.subtitle: You have successfully set up your first application, created your first event type, and subscribed to your first event in Hook0. Join us on {discord}, and we're also available on {github}.
congrats.feedback: To share your feedback or suggestions, visit our {discussions}. Stay updated through our {changelog} and take the best out of Hook0 by learning more at our {documentation}.

error404.title: This hook came up empty
error404.description: The page you're looking for doesn't exist or has been moved.
error404.maxRetries: Max retries exceeded. The page doesn't exist!

comingSoon.title: Coming soon...
comingSoon.description: This feature is coming soon. The API already supports it — check the docs to get started.

apiDocs.title: Hook0 API Documentation
apiDocs.subtitle: Automate everything!
apiDocs.description: Explore the full Hook0 API reference, including authentication, webhooks, events, and subscriptions.

footer.openSourceTagline: Open source always wins in the end.
